intro.title: Paris to Constantinople by Rail
intro.body: The Compagnie Internationale des Wagons-Lits inaugurated its through-train from Paris to Constantinople on 4 October 1883. The first run took 81 hours 40 minutes; passengers had to detrain at Giurgiu, ferry the Danube, and continue by Ottoman rail and steamer from Varna.

stops.name: Paris (Gare de l'Est)
stops.note: Departure 18:30, 4 October 1883. Inaugural train of 5 cars including the diner.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Gas-lit Belle Époque platform under a glass arch, locomotive of the Compagnie de l'Est venting steam, passengers in fur collars.

stops.name: Vienna
stops.note: Stop the morning of 5 October. The Austrian railways take over the train into Hungarian territory.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Viennese Westbahnhof at autumn dawn, Habsburg railway officials saluting on platform, train of dark blue carriages.

stops.name: Bucharest
stops.note: Arrival 6 October. The Romanian leg ends at Giurgiu where passengers disembark for the Danube ferry.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Bucharest station under autumn rain, passengers crossing the platform with porters, gilded cathedral roof in the distance.

stops.name: Varna
stops.note: Black Sea port. The Wagons-Lits steamer Espero takes passengers from Varna across the Black Sea to the Bosphorus.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Bulgarian port at sunset, side-wheel steamer with two funnels at the mole, passengers boarding with hand luggage.

stops.name: Constantinople (Sirkeci)
stops.note: Arrival 8 October at the Sirkeci terminus on the European shore, 81 hours from Paris.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Sirkeci terminus on the Marmara shore, ornate orientalist station building, Ottoman officers greeting first-class passengers.
